steps.title: Identify what the notation K(0) = 0 represents
steps.content: In $K(v) = \frac{1}{2}(41)(v^2)$, the input is $v$ (speed in meters per second) and the output is $K$ (kinetic energy in joules). Writing $K(0)$ means substituting $v = 0$ — a speed of 0 m/s.

steps.title: Compute K(0) to confirm the result
steps.content: $$K(0) = \frac{1}{2}(41)(0^2) = \frac{1}{2}(41)(0) = 0 \text{ joules}$$The equation $K(0) = 0$ literally says: when speed is 0 m/s, kinetic energy is 0 joules.

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• A) "Kinetic energy is 0 joules when mass is 0 kg" — mass is fixed at 41 kg and is not the function's input. Incorrect.
  • B) "Speed is 0 m/s when mass is 0 kg" — confuses input and output and misidentifies mass as a variable. Incorrect.
  • C) "Kinetic energy is 0 joules when the object travels at 0 m/s" — correctly identifies $v=0$ as the input and $K=0$ as the output. That matches choice C — C is correct.
  • D) "Height is 0 meters when speed is 0 m/s" — height appears nowhere in this function. Incorrect.
